summary: Name/README mismatch with unused runtime dependency on logger-active
details: intquery@1.1.4 publishes under a name that does not match its own README, which identifies the package as 'ts-logger-pack' and claims zero dependencies. The shipped dist/index.cjs only exposes an inert dummyLogger and does not import anything from the declared runtime dependency 'logger-active@^3.2.0'. Pulling in an unrelated, unused, unpinned (caret-range) runtime dependency that the package's own code never references is the structural shape used to smuggle behavior into installers via a transitive package — installing intquery silently resolves and installs logger-active into the consumer's tree even though nothing in intquery uses it. The intquery package itself contains no exfiltration, dropper, or install-time execution; the risk depends entirely on what logger-active does. Routing to human review so logger-active can be evaluated separately and the name/README mismatch can be assessed.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
